rationale: Both candidates independently catch the latent gap (the surviving get_cache_scale caller in hunyuan_image3_transformer.py that later became issue #4891), so gap_hit is true for both, and both are concrete/file-line actionable. X additionally reconstructs and validates the actual technical reasoning the human reviewer gave (delegated vs. direct loaders, why removing the manual branches is safe) and adds a grounded critique of the new test's real coverage, giving it noticeably higher recall against the ground-truth review; it does contain one small factual wobble (an odd '(see #4810)' self-reference). Y is narrower and more repetitive (the same three findings duplicated verbatim across retries), and its blocker framing ignores the human reviewer's note that DiT coverage was deliberately deferred to #4808, slightly mischaracterizing scope even though the underlying gap it flags is real.
